pages.title: Project Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to the Cost of the Tabernacle project: what it does, why it exists, its architecture pattern, and how the major components relate. Links to child pages for deeper coverage.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: How to run the site locally, open it via GitHub Pages, and understand the two published pages (calculator and history chart). Covers prerequisites and the file:// vs HTTP loading distinction.

pages.title: Methodology & Scope
pages.purpose: Explains the biblical scope (Exodus 38:21–39:41), weight-conversion assumptions (talent/shekel/hin to metric), the lower-bound total concept, and how cross-referenced Exodus passages are used. Covers METHODOLOGY.md in depth.

pages.title: Web Application
pages.purpose: Overview of the client-side static site hosted under docs/. Introduces the two pages and their shared CSS, then links to child pages for the calculator and history views.

pages.title: Live Calculator (index.html & app.js)
pages.purpose: Deep dive into the calculator page: DOM structure, UI controls (currency toggle, bronze-factor input, refresh button), the MATERIALS dataset, state object, refreshLiveQuotes(), buildRowView(), and render() functions. Covers the full data-flow from API fetch to DOM update.

pages.title: Historical Price Chart (history.html & history.js)
pages.purpose: Covers the history page: how history.js loads data (JS mirror first, JSON fetch fallback), Chart.js rendering, series-mode switching (total / per-material unit prices), currency toggle, and the latest-snapshot summary table.

pages.title: Styling & Assets
pages.purpose: Documents docs/styles.css (layout, card styles, table styling, responsive behavior, visual states for live/snapshot/gap rows) and the static asset (tabernacle-holman-1890.jpg).

pages.title: Data Layer
pages.purpose: Overview of how pricing data is stored and structured. Introduces the two data files (price-history.json and price-history.js mirror) and links to child pages for schema details and the automation pipeline.

pages.title: Price History Schema
pages.purpose: Detailed reference for the price-history.json / price-history.js data schema: version field, entries array, per-entry fields (recordedAt, fxDate, usdToEur, bronzeFactor, documentedTotalUsd/Eur), material identifiers, amountKind values, pricingBasis strings, null rowTotal semantics, and the JS mirror wrapper.

pages.title: Material Catalogue
pages.purpose: Reference table of all 16 tracked materials: their IDs, biblical verse citations, amount kinds, pricing types (live-metal, derived-bronze, snapshot), and current proxy sources. Explains the distinction between quantified and unspecified rows.

pages.title: Automation & Scripts
pages.purpose: Overview of the Python maintenance scripts and the GitHub Actions workflow that keep the data files current. Links to child pages for each script and the CI workflow.

pages.title: record-price-history.py
pages.purpose: Documents the monthly snapshot recorder: CLI arguments (--recorded-at, --replace-existing, --history-path), build_snapshot() logic, API calls, unit conversions, write_history_files() dual-output, and how it is invoked by GitHub Actions.

pages.title: backfill-price-history.py
pages.purpose: Documents the historical backfill utility: CLI flags (--years, --frequency, --reset-history), Yahoo Finance futures data fetching, Frankfurter historical FX series, monthly-first-day targeting, and how static proxy rows are handled during backfill.

pages.title: update-fallback-values.py
pages.purpose: Documents the fallback-refresh script: what FALLBACK_QUOTES is, how the script fetches fresh values from Gold-API and Frankfurter, the regex replacement strategy for patching app.js in-place, and when to run it.

pages.title: GitHub Actions Workflow
pages.purpose: Covers record-price-history.yml in detail: cron schedule (0 6 1 * *), workflow_dispatch trigger, schedule_guard bash logic, contents:write permission, Python 3.11 environment setup, and the git diff guard that prevents empty commits.

pages.title: External API Integrations
pages.purpose: Reference for all third-party data sources used by the project: Gold-API (XAU, XAG, HG symbols), Frankfurter (USD/EUR), and Yahoo Finance (historical backfill only). Covers unit conventions, failure handling, and the FALLBACK_QUOTES safety net.

pages.title: Live Price Feeds (Gold-API & Frankfurter)
pages.purpose: Details the Gold-API integration for XAU/XAG/HG spot quotes, troy-ounce and pound-to-kilogram conversions, the Frankfurter USD-to-EUR endpoint, and how the browser app and Python scripts share the same feed URLs.

pages.title: Historical Data Source (Yahoo Finance)
pages.purpose: Explains how backfill-price-history.py uses Yahoo Finance futures chart data for historical gold, silver, and copper prices, the date-alignment logic for first-of-month targeting, and why non-metal proxy rows remain static in the backfill.

pages.title: Contributing & Issue Tracking
pages.purpose: Overview of the contribution workflow, GitHub issue templates, and how to report bugs, documentation errors, pricing feed problems, or general enhancements.

pages.title: Bug & Enhancement Reports
pages.purpose: Documents the bugs.md and other.md issue templates: required fields (OS, Browser, Version, reproduction steps, expected behavior), label conventions ([BUG] and [ENHANCE] prefixes), and guidance on when to use each template.

pages.title: Documentation & Pricing Feed Issues
pages.purpose: Documents the documentation.md and pricing_feeds.md issue templates: the URL-reference requirement for documentation issues, the API URL field for pricing feed reports, and the pricefeeds label workflow.

pages.title: Glossary
pages.purpose: Definitions of all codebase-specific terms, biblical units, financial concepts, and abbreviations that an onboarding engineer needs to understand the project.
